title: Add VERILATED_TIMING MACRO
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

> What would you like added/supported?

As a maintainer of library which serves many users with different versions of Verilator, I would like a way to switch implementations based on version numbers. 

Specifically, we provide a clock generator which uses the standard #period/2 scheme: https://github.com/bespoke-silicon-group/basejump_stl/blob/master/bsg_test/bsg_nonsynth_clock_gen.v. Because v4 of Verilator did not support #delay, we also provide a DPI version of the clock generator which Verilator can drive.

For this, we currently have 
```
`ifdef VERILATOR
dpi_clock_gen
`else
delay_clock_gen
`endif
```

Since v5 of Verilator supports #delay, we would like this to look like:
```
`ifdef VERILATOR
`ifndef VERILATOR_V5
dpi_clock_gen
`else
delay_clock_gen
`else
delay_clock_gen
`endif
```

I suggest maybe:
VERILATOR_VERSION_MAJOR (5, 6, 7...)
VERILATOR_VERSION_MINOR (month of release? Not sure if there's an existing schema)
VERILATOR_COMMIT_ID

Apologies if some of these exist already, but I only found verilator3 in the docs: https://veripool.org/guide/latest/extensions.html.

If you dislike this scheme, a macro for VERILATOR_DELAY_SUPPORT would solve this problem, since it is the most disruptive (and exciting) change for v5.

> Can you attach an example that runs on other simulators?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

I'm not aware of commercial simulators providing this switch, but it's their loss :)

> May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Yes, if you point me to the correct place to add.
